_about: Self-play reward shaping. Sign convention: each field is the SIGNED reward contribution per fire (positive = reward, negative = penalty). Option-C-v2 tuning 2026-05-15: keep Option C's asymmetric kill credit + per-attack bonus, plus modest magnitude bump (attack_attempt_bonus 2x, damage_dealt 1.75x, per_turn_penalty 3.3x lower) to widen the return spread between engaged and disengaged trajectories. Overnight 121-iter local run on Option C showed no collapse + modest 0.72%->1.24% attack rise; this v2 is meant to ACCELERATE that climb. Per-game engagement budget: ~2 attacks * 0.95 = 1.9 + approach 0.2 - per_turn 0.06 ~ +2 vs ~-0.06 disengaged. 2026-05-17: enabled fog_reveal_weight=0.3 (continuous per-step payment proportional to currently-visible map fraction; gamma must match trainer to deliver weight as the total discounted return for a fully-explored sustained-visibility game). Targets the long-march regime mini-maps don't exercise; near-zero contribution on mini-maps where vision saturates immediately. fog_reveal_gamma=0.99 mirrors TrainerConfig.gamma; tune both together.
_per_turn_penalty_note: User directive 2026-05-20: per-step costs disabled. The penalty fires per Python-side action (despite its name); the only behavior change it would incentivize is 'lump many one-hex moves into one big move', which isn't a priority. Keeping the FIELD at 0 (not removing) so old configs warning-on-load instead of hard-erroring if the key resurfaces.
_approach_metric_note: Renamed 2026-05-20: approach_enemy_leader_per_hex (hex-distance delta) -> approach_enemy_leader_per_mp (terrain-aware MP-cost delta via Dijkstra). The hex metric under-credited routing around passable obstacles (mountain pass, forest detour) even when MP-equivalent; the MP metric is what the policy actually pays. Same weight 0.01 keeps cumulative reward magnitude close to before on flat-heavy maps; rough maps get slightly more reward for the same path because MP > hex distance there.
